recapPrompt.prompt: Why do many graph problems need a visited set?

choices.label: To stop repeated looping through old nodes
choices.feedback: Correct. Graphs can connect back to old nodes, so visited memory keeps the walk safe.

choices.label: To sort the nodes from smallest to largest
choices.feedback: Not this one. Visited memory is about safe traversal, not sorting.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Explain in your own words why Number of Islands is really a graph idea hiding inside a grid.
reflectionPrompt.placeholder: It is really a graph idea because...

practiceChallenge.title: Mastery check: rebuild the graph clues
practiceChallenge.description: Complete the helper so it returns the main clue for path questions, group questions, and dependency-cycle questions.
hints: Path questions ask whether one node can reach another.
Group questions ask how many separate connected parts exist.
Cycle questions ask whether a loop blocks the plan.
practiceChallenge.successMessage: Pass. You rebuilt the main graph clues from memory.
practiceChallenge.retryMessage: Needs practice. Recheck path, group, and dependency-cycle graph clues.
